episode.title: Contributor Onboarding — Ep 0 — What AeroVista Is

content.summary_short: AeroVista is a multi-division ecosystem that ships small, real releases with clean lanes and safe defaults.
content.summary_long: Start with the shape of the system: AeroVista is an ecosystem across apps, music, branding, and infrastructure. The point is not heroic output. The point is clean, repeatable releases that strengthen the whole studio over time.

transcript: AeroVista is not a single app, a single track, or a single brand page. It is a multi-division ecosystem where apps, music, visuals, and infrastructure support each other.

The operating idea is simple: ship small drops that are real, reviewable, and repeatable. A release can be a song, a landing page, a tool, a workflow, or a support document, as long as it strengthens the system instead of adding noise.
That is why the culture emphasizes local-first builds, security by default, and quality as a form of respect. Every drop should feel intentional, not rushed and not vague.
In this environment, big heroic commits are less valuable than small proof-backed wins. Progress compounds when each release leaves the ecosystem clearer than it found it.

This training pack turns that operating model into a contributor path: understand the studio, check for mutual fit, learn the work lanes, use work orders and Definition of Done, then prove readiness through the Crew Check flow.

slides.title: One ecosystem, many outputs
slides.body: AeroVista connects apps, music, branding, and infrastructure instead of treating them like unrelated projects.
notes: A drop can be a song, a tool, a launch page, or an ops support asset.
Each release should make the whole system stronger, not just finish one task.
Contributors work better when they understand the ecosystem context first.

slides.title: Small drops keep momentum honest
slides.body: The studio favors small, reviewable releases because they are easier to test, explain, and repeat.
notes: One task should map to one change set and one clear outcome.

slides.title: Craft plus care
slides.body: Quality, security, and story are part of the same standard. Fast is fine, but only when clean.
notes: Quality is respect for users and for the team.
The work should still feel human, not churned out.

slides.title: The contributor path
slides.body: This pack moves from orientation to readiness so work starts with clarity instead of improvisation.
notes: Fit Check filters for working style.
Lanes define what is safe, what needs review, and what is restricted.
Work orders and Definition of Done turn expectations into proof.

engage.prompt: Which statement matches AeroVista's operating model?

options.label: It is an ecosystem across multiple divisions, and each small release should strengthen the whole system.
It is mainly a place for large refactors because big changes show commitment.
It is a marketing-only brand site, so contributor rules matter less than speed.
